watermelon-and-snap-pea-salad.name: Watermelon And Snap Pea Salad

ingredients.item: Kong yen or chinkiang black vinegar
. black sesame oil or toasted sesame oil
. chile crisp (such as lao gan ma) or sze daddy chili sauce
Taiwanese or japanese groundblack sugar or dark brown sugar
Freshly ground white or black pepper

ingredients.prep: trimmed, thinly sliced on a diagonal

ingredients.item: Thinly sliced thai basil or mint

ingredients.prep: rind removed, flesh cut into 1" pieces

ingredients.item: . toasted black and/or white sesame seeds

instructions: Mix garlic, vinegar, oil, chile crisp, soy sauce, fish sauce, black sugar, white pepper, and 1 Tbsp. fried shallots in a medium bowl until sugar is dissolved.
Add sugar snap peas and basil to dressing in bowl and toss to coat.

Arrange watermelon on a platter or individual plates and top with sugar snap pea mixture. Drizzle any dressing remaining in bowl over. Scatter sesame seeds and remaining 2 Tbsp. fried shallots on top.

watermelon-sugar-cocktail.name: Watermelon Sugar Cocktail
instructions: Scrape stems, seeds, and veins from jalapeño into a small jar (reserve green flesh for another use). Add tequila; cover and let infuse for 15 minutes. Set a strainer over a small bowl. Pour tequila through strainer and discard solids.

Place watermelon cubes in a large pitcher. Using a muddler or potato masher, coarsely crush watermelon. Stir in lime juice, Simple Syrup, mezcal, and jalapeño tequila. Stir in 2 cups ice. Cover and refrigerate until chilled, about 2 hours.
Fill Old Fashioned glasses with ice. Divide cocktail among glasses and serve.

wild-boar-in-hull-hunting-argiro.name: Wild Boar In Hull (hunting)

ingredients.item: 4 medium onions cut into thick slices
1 branch of fresh thyme or rosemary
For the purposes of this chapter:
A little flour for the breading
2 okra beans and a few pepper beans
4 medium onions cut into thick slices
1 branch of fresh thyme or rosemary
For the purposes of this chapter:
A little flour for the breading
2 okra beans and a few pepper beans

instructions: We start the process to make the boar in the hull, washing the meat first.
Cut it into rations.
To remove a heavy smell, add it from the morning of the previous day in a bowl with 1/3 cup rosemary vinegar and 1 tablespoon thick salt.
Leave it in the fridge for several hours. In the evening rinse it from the drier and place it in a fireproof vessel that is well covered.
Pour it with the blackwash and balsamic.
Add the bay leaf, orange peel, thyme, parsley, spice beans and pepper granules.
We cover it well.
Leave it in the fridge one night, marinated, on the one hand to soften and on the other to water and taste the aromas of marinade.
The next day we drain the hunting from the marinata and wipe it with absorbent paper.
We're holding the marinade.
Heat 1/2 olive oil from the total over medium heat, in a pot that enters the kitchen eye.
Add the onions and garlic and let them become transparent and slightly blond, stirring frequently.
In a pan, heat 2-3 tablespoons of olive oil over high heat.
Bake the hunt in some flour and shake well to remove the unnecessary.
Add a few pieces of the meat so that the temperature of the oil does not fall.
Each piece of red-red meat is poured into the onions.
After all the meat is browned, put it in the onion pot.
Add the paste and pour with the marinade.
Add salt and pepper, let it take a bottle and place it in a well-heated oven, covered, at 170°C for an hour and a half.
Then, check the salt and pepper, add the chestnuts and plums and let it cook for an extra 30 minutes until it softens well.
We're checking if it was baked before we took it off.
The meat must be very tender and soft after baking.

winter-squash-carbonara-with-pancetta-an.name: Winter Squash Carbonara With Pancetta And Sage

ingredients.prep: peeled, seeded, cut into  0.5” pieces (about 3 cups)

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add pancetta, reduce heat to medium, and cook, stirring occasionally, until crisp, 8–10 minutes. Add sage and toss to coat. Using a slotted spoon, transfer pancetta and sage to a small bowl; set aside.

Add squash, onion, and garlic to skillet; season with salt and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is translucent, 8–10 minutes. Add broth. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer until squash is soft and liquid is reduced by half, 15–20 minutes. Let cool slightly, then purée in a blender until smooth; season with salt and pepper. Reserve skillet.
Cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente. Drain, reserving 1 cup pasta cooking liquid.

Combine pasta, squash purée, and ¼ cup pasta cooking liquid in reserved skillet and cook over medium heat, tossing and adding more pasta cooking liquid as needed, until sauce coats pasta, about 2 minutes. Mix in ¼ cup Pecorino; season with salt and pepper.
Serve pasta topped with reserved pancetta and sage, shaved Pecorino, and more pepper.
DO AHEAD: Squash purée can be made 3 days ahead. Let cool; cover and chill.
